# SQLite (vendored)

[SQLite](https://www.sqlite.org) 3.53.3 source
(`https://sqlite.org/2026/sqlite-amalgamation-3530300.zip`), vendored as an
unmodified extraction of the upstream amalgamation release — every file
under `sqlite-3.53.3/` is byte-for-byte identical to what `sqlite.org`
ships. No SQLite source file is patched for this port. `LICENSE` here is
SQLite's public-domain dedication, copied verbatim from the header comment
every file in the release begins with (SQLite has never shipped a
separate `LICENSE` file — the dedication only exists as that header text
and on sqlite.org's own copyright page).

SQLite is built directly by PureUNIX's own top-level `Makefile` (see the
`SQLite 3.53.3` section there), the same "vendor upstream source, compile
it with our own Makefile rules, no upstream build system" pattern already
used for `third_party/tcc/` and `third_party/lua/`. One ELF binary comes
out of this: `/bin/sqlite3`, the real upstream command-line shell, linked
directly against the real upstream core library — both from the same
amalgamation — installed on the persistent EXT2 image
(`docs/sqlite-port.md`).

## Why the amalgamation, not `sqlite-src-*.zip`

SQLite's own "urtext" source (`sqlite-src-*.zip`, referenced from the same
download page) is hundreds of individual `.c`/`.h` files assembled by a
Tcl-based build system (`configure`/`Makefile.in`, itself generated from
a Tcl `translate` step) that assumes a hosted, Autotools-capable build
environment nothing here provides. The **amalgamation** —
`sqlite3.c` + `sqlite3.h` + `sqlite3ext.h` + `shell.c` — is not a
third-party repackaging or a simplification PureUNIX chose: it is
SQLite's own official release artifact, generated by the SQLite project
itself from that same urtext source and distributed at the exact same
`sqlite.org/download.html` as the primary, recommended way to embed
SQLite in another project ("This is all you need to compile SQLite," the
amalgamation's own top-of-file comment says). Using it is not a departure
from "real upstream source" — it *is* the real upstream source, in the
form upstream itself packages for exactly this kind of build.

## Why not SQLite's own build system

Even setting the urtext-vs-amalgamation question aside, the amalgamation
was never meant to be built by a project-specific `configure`/`Makefile`
either — its own documented build recipe is literally
`gcc shell.c sqlite3.c -o sqlite3` (plus `-ldl -lpthread` on a hosted
Unix). PureUNIX's Makefile reproduces exactly that: two `.c` files
compiled with `i686-elf-gcc` and PureUNIX's own newlib-linked-program
rules (`NEWLIB_CFLAGS`, `newlib_crt0`/`newlib_syscalls.o`), no `-ldl`
(no dynamic linker exists at all) and no `-lpthread` (no threading model
exists — see `-DSQLITE_THREADSAFE=0` below).

## Compile-time configuration

No SQLite source is patched; every platform decision is a documented,
upstream-supported `-D` flag, the same "declare platform intent
explicitly" style as Lua's `-DLUA_USE_POSIX` / `-DLUA_USE_DLOPEN`
omission:

| Define | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `SQLITE_OS_UNIX=1` | Forces the real unix VFS backend (`os_unix.c`) explicitly, rather than relying on `sqlite3.c`'s own `#if defined(_WIN32) ... #else unix #endif` autodetection (which already lands on unix here, since nothing defines `_WIN32` on this freestanding target — this is just explicit instead of incidental). |
| `SQLITE_THREADSAFE=0` | No threading model exists (same reasoning as Lua's `flockfile()`/`funlockfile()` no-ops) — SQLite's internal mutex layer compiles out entirely instead of needing a pthread port. |
| `SQLITE_OMIT_LOAD_EXTENSION` | No dynamic linker exists at all (same reasoning as Lua's `LUA_USE_DLOPEN` omission / TCC's `CONFIG_TCC_STATIC`) — `.load`/extension loading fails cleanly with SQLite's own upstream "not authorized" message; every other SQL feature is unaffected. |
| `SQLITE_OMIT_WAL` | WAL mode needs a real shared-memory VFS layer (`xShmMap`/`xShmLock`, normally an mmap'd `-shm` file coordinating multiple processes) this port doesn't implement. The default rollback-journal mode — what every `sqlite3 file.db` session uses unless it explicitly runs `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` — needs none of that and works fully. |
| `SQLITE_MAX_MMAP_SIZE=0` | PureUNIX's `mmap()` (`user/newlib_syscalls.c`) only supports `MAP_ANON\|MAP_PRIVATE` scratch allocations, not a real file-backed mapping SQLite's opportunistic mmap I/O path would need — this disables that path cleanly instead of letting it fail at runtime. |
| `HAVE_USLEEP=1` | Real `usleep()` exists (added for the Lua port) — lets `unixSleep()` use real microsecond-resolution sleeps for the SQL-layer busy-handler retry loop instead of rounding every retry up to a whole second. |

See `docs/sqlite-port.md` for what platform work these flags' real
implementations needed (most importantly `fcntl(F_SETLK/F_GETLK)`) and a
genuine, general PureUNIX kernel bug this port found and fixed along the
way.
